news.title: Projects worth ₹31,000 crore approved under Urban Challenge Fund: MoHUA official
news.para: Projects worth ₹31,000 crore have been approved under the Urban Challenge Fund within 20-25 days of its guidelines being finalised, said D Thara, additional secretary, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs. NBFCs account for 44% of the financing for approved projects.

news.title: John Abraham buys 100-year-old Bandra bungalow for ₹84 crore; ‘no redevelopment plans’
news.para: Actor John Abraham recently bought a historic bungalow in Bandra West for eighty-four crore rupees. This property, built in nineteen twenty-six, was owned by the DeVitre family. Abraham also purchased a Linking Road bungalow in Khar for seventy-five crore rupees last December. Many Bollywood personalities invest heavily in Mumbai real estate for personal and investment reasons. Celebrities often prefer properties in the Bandra to Andheri suburbs.

news.title: Redevelopment agreement not a property receipt, so not taxable: ITAT
news.para: Mumbai ITAT ruled agreement registration does not mean property receipt.  Tax cannot be levied on future property rights under anti-abuse provisions.  The tribunal allowed a taxpayer's appeal against a ₹1.38 crore tax addition.  Registration creates contractual rights, not actual property possession or receipt.  This decision clarifies tax treatment for ongoing redevelopment projects in Mumbai.

news.title: Gurugram civic body faces property ID backlog, over 5,000 applications yet to be processed
news.para: Gurugram's civic body faces thousands of pending property ID objections and applications.  Many cases remain with the MCG, while others are returned to residents for documents.  Fresh property ID applications also show significant delays at the civic body's level.  Recent staff transfers followed corruption allegations and file tampering concerns.  This situation prolongs the process for property owners seeking record corrections.

news.title: Haryana government restores ban on stilt-plus-four building approvals
news.para: Haryana's town planning department has stopped new stilt-plus-four building approvals statewide. This decision follows proceedings before the Punjab and Haryana High Court on Wednesday. The department cited an unfinished framework for online building plan approvals and administrative mechanisms. All pending and fresh applications for these buildings will not be approved. The statewide freeze remains until a regulatory framework is established or court orders change.

news.title: KFin Technologies leases over 2.42 lakh sq ft office in Hyderabad’s Kokapet
news.para: KFin Technologies has leased over 2.42 lakh sq ft of office space at Rajapushpa West Avenue in Hyderabad's Kokapet through five lease agreements with Rajapushpa Realty LLP.

news.title: Schindler's revenue falls slightly to $3.38 billion in Q2 2026
news.para: Schindler's second-quarter sales fell short of market expectations, impacting its stock price. A sluggish Chinese market and currency effects significantly contributed to this disappointing performance. New installation orders in China experienced a notable decline of over ten percent. The company also anticipates additional inflation-related costs this year from rising prices.  Competitors Kone and Otis will soon release their financial results.

news.title: Gurugram: Orris Infrastructure, its directors booked for cheating in land agreement
news.para: Gurugram police filed a cheating case against Orris Infrastructure and its directors.  Complainants allege fraud in a land agreement involving agricultural property near Manesar.  The builder allegedly transferred allotted plots to third parties despite prior agreements.  Legal heirs discovered plot transfers and construction on their land this year.  Investigation is underway to verify facts and take legal action.

news.title: Sunteck Realty's net profit rises 26% to ₹42 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Sunteck Realty reported a 26 percent net profit decline for the June quarter.  Its consolidated net profit stood at Rs 41.96 crore during this period.  Total income rose marginally to Rs 202.02 crore in the first quarter.  Operational expenses declined to Rs 150 crore from Rs 159 crore.  This Mumbai-based developer is a leading real estate entity.

news.title: Delhi government to soon provide smart property cards in Lal Dora areas of 48 villages: CM
news.para: Delhi government is preparing digital property records for Lal Dora areas.  Smart property cards will soon be issued to eligible owners.  Surveys have been completed in many villages, and property cards are ready.  This initiative aims to resolve long-standing property record issues for residents.  The digital system will enhance transparency and security of property information.

news.title: Divyansh Group acquires Ghaziabad land parcel for ₹128 crore
news.para: Divyansh Group has acquired a 12,868 sq metre land parcel in Koyal Enclave, Ghaziabad, through a Ghaziabad Development Authority auction. The company plans to develop a plotted residential project with sales potential of over ₹200 crore.

news.title: Institutional investments in real estate decline 7% YoY to $1.9 billion in Q2 2026: Report
news.para: Institutional investments in Indian real estate stood at $1.9 billion in Q2 2026, down 7% year-on-year, according to Cushman & Wakefield. Office assets accounted for 51% of total investments, while data centres contributed 40%.

news.title: NAKSHA will not affect property ownership documents, boundaries: Goa government
news.para: Existing property titles and land records remain completely secure and unaffected.  The NAKSHA pilot project is a data-mapping exercise for urban land parcels.  Rights of non-resident landowners are fully safeguarded during the survey.  Physical absence does not impact legal ownership or property rights.  This initiative aims to modernize land administration and enhance town planning.

news.title: Any deed related to properties can be registered within Goa districts
news.para: Goa citizens can now register property documents at any district sub-registrar office. This new system allows registration anywhere within the district, not just taluka-level offices. The state government will amend the Registration Act to facilitate this district-wide registration process. Online document uploads will show pendency, enabling faster appointment selection. This initiative aims to improve ease of doing business for property transactions.

news.title: Lucknow civic body to launch one-time settlement scheme for house tax defaulters
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation will launch a one-time settlement scheme for tax defaulters starting August 15. This initiative offers a chance to clear pending house, water, and sewer taxes up to April 2026. Taxpayers paying the principal amount will receive waivers on accumulated interest and surcharges. Those with dues over one lakh rupees can pay in three monthly installments. The scheme aims to improve recoveries and strengthen the corporation's financial position.

news.title: Surcharge on property transactions likely as Jharkhand government eyes revenue boost for civic bodies
news.para: Jharkhand's government plans a new ten percent surcharge on property transfers. This levy aims to increase revenue for municipal bodies across the state. The proposed surcharge will apply to stamp duty and registration fees for property sales. Funds collected will support civic bodies' revenue and capital expenditures. Public feedback on the draft rules is open until July twenty-fourth.

news.title: Telangana launches Indiramma housing scheme for poor in Hyderabad Core Urban Region
news.para: The Telangana government has launched the Indiramma housing scheme for the poor. This initiative will provide one lakh houses in Hyderabad's Core Urban Region. Each flat will have a built-up area of 525 square feet. Beneficiaries will receive a Rs 5 lakh subsidy from the government. The scheme aims to construct multi-storey buildings for eligible citizens.

news.title: MahaRERA recovery warrant dashboard not updated for six months, activists question transparency
news.para: Consumer activists question MahaRERA's transparency regarding its frozen dashboard.  The online tracking system for defaulting developers has remained un-updated for six months.  Homebuyers cannot track enforcement actions and recoveries effectively.  Activists urge prompt data updates and better accountability from the regulator.  They have also sought intervention from the revenue minister for faster warrant execution.

news.title: Bombay HC nixes auction of four-acre land in housing project in Shikrapur
news.para: The Bombay High Court annulled a land auction held on April 29.  It directed the project developer to refund Rs 3.15 crore to 13 flat purchasers.  The court also ordered action against the Shirur tehsildar for auction irregularities.  The Pune district collector faced criticism for his casual court appearance.  This ruling aims to resolve a long-standing dispute for homebuyers.

news.title: Redevelopment in Ahmedabad hits cost, consent roadblock
news.para: Redevelopment projects in Ahmedabad are experiencing significant delays.  Weak housing demand and rising construction costs deter builders from new commitments.  Disagreements among society members also halt progress on many stalled projects.  Some families are now paying their own rent for vacant homes.  Developers urge complete consensus and proven builder track records for success.
